## Code Review

This pull request introduces several improvements and fixes across the application, including stricter course validation, robust base64 encoding/decoding for sharing, improved geolocation tracking, and better wake lock management. Key feedback highlights that the new `playedPar` helper incorrectly includes the active hole's par during active rounds, which can be resolved by only summing completed holes on the scorecard. Additionally, the Semgrep rule pattern for GitHub Actions permissions should be updated to match any defined permissions block, and status message updates in `CourseCreator.svelte` should be refined to prevent overwriting active placement instructions or showing disruptive geolocation errors.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The pattern `permissions: {}` only matches an empty permissions block. If a workflow defines explicit permissions (e.g., `contents: read`), it will not match this pattern and will be falsely flagged as violating the rule. Use `permissions: ...` to match any defined permissions block.

```yaml
      - pattern-not-inside: |
          permissions: ...
          ...
```

**2 issues found** across 18 files
Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P3: The Semgrep rule's `pattern-inside` only matches the mapping-form event trigger (`on: pull_request: ...`). A workflow using the equally valid shorthand `on: [pull_request]` or `on: pull_request` would bypass detection entirely. Consider adding an alternative pattern using `pattern-either:` to cover both the mapping form and the array/scalar shorthand forms. P3: The `allowBuilds` configuration in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` won't take effect with the current pnpm version. The project's `pnpm-lock.yaml` uses `lockfileVersion: '9.0'` (pnpm v9), but `allowBuilds` was only added in pnpm v10.26.0 and is silently ignored by v9. Consider either: (a) adding the equivalent pnpm v9 config via `.npmrc` or `package.json`'s `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` if the intent is to restrict build scripts now, or (b) noting in the file or PR description that this config is forward-compatible for a planned pnpm upgrade. Otherwise, anyone running `pnpm install` with v9 won't have build-script permissions enforced as the config suggests. ```
**Tip**: cubic can generate docs of your entire codebase and keep them up to date. Try it [here](https://docs.cubic.dev/wiki/ai-wiki?utm_source=github).

P3: The Semgrep rule's `pattern-inside` only matches the mapping-form event trigger (`on:
  pull_request: ...`). A workflow using the equally valid shorthand `on: [pull_request]` or `on: pull_request` would bypass detection entirely. Consider adding an alternative pattern using `pattern-either:` to cover both the mapping form and the array/scalar shorthand forms.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At .semgrep/github-actions-permissions.yml, line 12:

<comment>The Semgrep rule's `pattern-inside` only matches the mapping-form event trigger (`on:
  pull_request: ...`). A workflow using the equally valid shorthand `on: [pull_request]` or `on: pull_request` would bypass detection entirely. Consider adding an alternative pattern using `pattern-either:` to cover both the mapping form and the array/scalar shorthand forms.</comment>

<file context>
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+        - /.github/workflows/*.yml
+        - /.github/workflows/*.yaml
+    patterns:
+      - pattern-inside: |
+          on:
+            pull_request: ...
</file context>
```

</details>

<!-- cubic:v=783472b7-98ac-454c-90a3-2bdbfef59d7d -->

P3: The `allowBuilds` configuration in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` won't take effect with the current pnpm version. The project's `pnpm-lock.yaml` uses `lockfileVersion: '9.0'` (pnpm v9), but `allowBuilds` was only added in pnpm v10.26.0 and is silently ignored by v9. Consider either: (a) adding the equivalent pnpm v9 config via `.npmrc` or `package.json`'s `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` if the intent is to restrict build scripts now, or (b) noting in the file or PR description that this config is forward-compatible for a planned pnpm upgrade. Otherwise, anyone running `pnpm install` with v9 won't have build-script permissions enforced as the config suggests.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents</summary>

```text
Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At pnpm-workspace.yaml, line 1:

<comment>The `allowBuilds` configuration in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` won't take effect with the current pnpm version. The project's `pnpm-lock.yaml` uses `lockfileVersion: '9.0'` (pnpm v9), but `allowBuilds` was only added in pnpm v10.26.0 and is silently ignored by v9. Consider either: (a) adding the equivalent pnpm v9 config via `.npmrc` or `package.json`'s `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` if the intent is to restrict build scripts now, or (b) noting in the file or PR description that this config is forward-compatible for a planned pnpm upgrade. Otherwise, anyone running `pnpm install` with v9 won't have build-script permissions enforced as the config suggests.</comment>

<file context>
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+allowBuilds:
+  '@biomejs/biome': true
+  esbuild: true
</file context>
```

</details>
